real_world_scenario.name: Enterprise Copilot with Mixed Interactive and Long-Context Requests
real_world_scenario.description: This workload models a production copilot where short conversational turns, follow-up clarifications, and rewrite requests arrive continuously while a smaller number of document-grounded long-context jobs are also admitted. Examples include support copilots, internal knowledge assistants, and document QA systems that mix chat turns with report summarization, meeting QA, and multi-hop retrieval.
why_real: Short interactive turns are well represented by open chat corpora such as UltraChat.
Long-context jobs are well represented by LongBench tasks such as report summarization and question answering over long contexts.
Poisson arrivals approximate independent user arrivals and are widely used to stress online serving systems under bursty but memoryless traffic.
The beneficiary-rich mixed ordering reflects realistic moments where long jobs begin slightly earlier and later short turns compete for responsiveness.

models.label: Mistral-7B-v0.1
models.reason: Base open-weight Mistral chat-capable model used as the family anchor.

models.label: Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2
models.reason: Instruction-tuned sibling of the same family to test whether gains survive instruction tuning.

models.label: Zephyr-7B-Beta
models.reason: Alignment-tuned Mistral derivative with different chat behavior and tokenizer usage.

models.label: OpenChat-3.5-0106
models.reason: A different Mistral-based chat stack that checks whether results survive another serving-oriented fine-tuning line.

models.label: Gemma-7B-IT
models.reason: Non-Mistral architecture with different attention and tokenizer behavior; strongest prior beneficiary-rich case.

models.label: Baichuan2-7B-Chat
models.reason: A non-Mistral, non-Gemma chat model with different runtime behavior and tokenizer characteristics, used to keep the suite diverse after excluding invalid LUT models.

optional_model_extensions.label: DeciLM-7B
optional_model_extensions.reason: Useful extension when testing another architecture/runtime mix with trust_remote_code enabled.

datasets.label: UltraChat200k
datasets.reason: Provides short and medium-length conversational user turns that resemble online assistant traffic.

datasets.label: LongBench
datasets.reason: Provides long prompts such as report summarization and long-context QA, matching document-heavy enterprise workloads.

optional_dataset_extensions.label: WildChat
optional_dataset_extensions.reason: Useful when stressing more heterogeneous user-facing short-turn traffic beyond curated assistant data.
optional_dataset_extensions.note: Requires adding a dataset extractor before use.

optional_dataset_extensions.label: InfiniteBench
optional_dataset_extensions.reason: Useful when the paper needs a stronger long-context stress test beyond the current LongBench mix.
optional_dataset_extensions.note: Requires adding a dataset extractor before use.

densities.name: low
densities.reason: Represents periods where concurrency exists but the system is not persistently saturated, so any gain should come from better responsiveness rather than forced queueing.

densities.name: mid
densities.reason: Represents the operating band where interactive queries and long jobs overlap frequently enough to expose meaningful tradeoffs without fully saturating the server.

densities.name: high
densities.reason: Represents burstier high-load intervals where the control policy must preserve responsiveness without letting wall time regress too sharply.
